properties.title: Owen Tudor is born Anglesay Wales 1400AD
properties.content: in the year 1400AD Owen Tudor is born on the island of Anglesay in Wales
properties.attribution: Sodacan, CC BY-SA 3.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/3.0>, via Wikimedia Commons

properties.title: Catherine of Valois is born Hotel Saint-Pol France 1401AD
properties.content: in the year 1401AD Catherine of Valois is born in Hotel St Pol France
properties.attribution: Caxton Master, "The Beauchamp Pageants", Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

properties.title: Owen and Catherine wed England 1428AD
properties.content: in the year 1428 in England Owen Tudor and Catherine of Valois Wed
properties.attribution: Sodacan, CC BY-SA 3.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/3.0>, via Wikimedia Commons

properties.title: Henry tudor defeats Richard III and becomes king Leicestershire England 1485AD.
properties.content: in the year 1485 Henry Tudor defeats Richard III at the battle of Bosworth Field and Becomes King Henry VII of England
properties.attribution: National Portrait Gallery, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

properties.title: Henry VII marries Elizabeth of York Westminster Abbey England 1486AD
properties.content: In the year 1486 AD Henry VII Marries Elizabeth of York at Westminster Abbey in England
properties.attribution: Unidentified painter, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

properties.title: Margaret Marries James IV of Scotland Edinburgh Scotland 1503AD
properties.content: in the year 1503AD Margaret Tudor Marries James IV of Scotland in Edinbourough Scotland
properties.attribution: The author died in 1640, so this work is in the public domain in its country of origin and other countries and areas where the copyright term is the author's life plus 100 years or fewer.

properties.title: Henry VIII Marries Catherine of Aragon Greenwich Palace England 1509AD
properties.content: in the year 1509AD Henry VIII marries his first wife Catherine of Aragon at Greenwich Palace in England
properties.attribution: Lambeth Palace, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

properties.title: Henry VII involves himself in the French-Breton war Brittany France 1489AD
properties.content: in the year 1489AD Henry VII Invades Brittany as part of the French-Breton war
properties.attribution: Unidentified painter, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

properties.title: Henry VII invades Scotland Scotland 1496AD
properties.content: In the year 1496AD Henry VII invades Scotland for revenge of the Scottish support for Perkin Warbeck
properties.attribution: See page for author, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

properties.title: Henry VIII is crowned king Westminster Abbey 1509AD
properties.content: In the year 1509AD Henry VII is crowned king at Westminster Abbey
properties.attribution: The author died in 1543, so this work is in the public domain in its country of origin and other countries and areas where the copyright term is the author's life plus 100 years or fewer.

properties.title: Catherine of Aragon gives birth to Mary Greenwich England 1516AD
properties.content: in the year 1516AD Catherine of Aragon gives birth to "Bloody" Mary at Greenwich Palace
properties.attribution: Michael Sittow, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

properties.title: Thomas Cromwell is Appointed Chief Minister England 1532AD
properties.content: in the year 1532AD Thomas Cromwell is Appointed to the position of Chief Minister
properties.attribution: Attributed to Hans Holbein the Younger, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

properties.title: Henry VIII breaks ties with Rome  England 1536AD
properties.content: In the year 1536AD Henry VIII Breaks ties with Rome and the Vatican because they Will not grant him an anulment
properties.attribution: John Closterman (1660-1711), Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

properties.title: Henry VIII divorces Catherine England 1533AD
properties.content: in the year 1533AD Henry VIII divorces Catherine of Aragon because of her failure to provide him with a son
properties.attribution: See page for author, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

properties.title: Henry VIII Marries Anne Boleyn Whitehall Palace England 1533AD
properties.content: In the year 1533AD Henry VIII weds for the second time this time he weds Anne Boleyn in Whitehall Palace
properties.attribution: William Powell Frith, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

properties.title: Anne Gives Birth to Elizabeth Greenwich England 1533AD
properties.content: in the year 1533AD Anne Gives birth to Elizabeth I in the Greenwich England
properties.attribution: George Cruikshank, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

properties.title: Anne is Arrested Greenwich England 1536AD
properties.content: In the year 1536AD Anne Boleyn is Arrested and charged with treason and incest based on the accusation that she engaged in cardinal relations with her brother
properties.attribution: Édouard Cibot, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

properties.title: Anne is Executed Tower of London England 1536AD
properties.content: in the year 1536AD Anne Boleyn is found guilty of Incest and Treason and beheaded at the Tower of London
properties.attribution: Robert Edwards / St. Mary's church, Erwarton, Suffolk

properties.title: Henry VIII Marries Jane Seymore Whitehall Palace England 1536AD
properties.content: In the year 1536AD Henry VIII weds for the third time this time to Jane Seymour at Whitehall palace
properties.attribution: Public domain The author died in 1543, so this work is in the public domain in its country of origin and other countries and areas where the copyright term is the author's life plus 100 years or fewer.

properties.title: Jane Seymore Births Prince Edward Hampton Court Palace England 1537AD
properties.content: in the year 1537AD Jane gives birth to Edward VI the first and only male heir of Henry VIII
properties.attribution: Hampton Court Palace, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

properties.title: Jane Seymore Dies Hampton Court Palace England 1537AD
properties.content: in the year 1537AD during the birth of Edward VI Jane Seymore dies
properties.attribution: Hans Holbein Workshop, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

properties.title: Henry VIII marries Anne of Cleves Greenwich England 1540AD
properties.content: in the year 1540AD Henry VIII Weds for a fourth time this time to Anne of Cleaves in Greenwich England
properties.attribution: This work is in the public domain in its country of origin and other countries and areas where the copyright term is the author's life plus 100 years or fewer.

properties.title: Henry VIII Divorces Anne of Cleves England 1540AD
properties.content: in the year 1540AD shortly after their wedding Henry VIII divorces Anne of Cleaves and gives her the title "My Lady, the King's Sister
properties.attribution: Hans Holbein the Younger, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

properties.title: Henry VIII Beheads Cromwell Tower Hill England 1540AD
properties.content: In the year 1540AD Thomas Cromwell is blamed for the Henry's 4th failed marraige and is beheaded on Tower Hill
properties.attribution: Mariordo (Mario Roberto Duran Ortiz), CC BY-SA 3.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/3.0>, via Wikimedia Commons

properties.title: Henry VIII Marries Catherine Howard Surrey England 1540AD
properties.content: in the year 1540AD Henry VIII weds for the 5th time this time to Catherine Howard in Surrey England
properties.attribution: Hans Holbein the Younger, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

properties.title: Henry VIII Executes Catherine Howard Tower of London England 1542AD
properties.content: in the year 1542AD Henry VIII Beheads Catherine Howard for alleged "Infidelity"
properties.attribution: VCR Giulio19, CC BY-SA 4.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/4.0>, via Wikimedia Commons

properties.title: Henry VIII Marries Catherine Parr Hampton Court Palace England 1543AD
properties.content: in the year 1543 Henry VIII marries for a 6th and final time this time to Catherine Parr in Hampton Court Palace England
properties.attribution: National Portrait Gallery, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

properties.title: Henry VIII dies Whitehall Palace England 1547AD
properties.content: in the year 1547AD Henry VIII dies in Whitehall Palace
properties.attribution: Alfred Young Nutt, Surveyor to the Dean and Canons of St George's Chapel, Windsor Castle, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

properties.title: Edward VI is crowned king Westminster Abbey England 1547AD
properties.content: In the year 1547AD Edward VI is crowned king at Westminster Abbey
properties.attribution: This work is in the public domain in its country of origin and other countries and areas where the copyright term is the author's life plus 100 years or fewer.

properties.title: Edward VI's regent invades Scotland Scotland 1547AD
properties.content: In the year 1547AD Edward VI's Regent Edward Seymour invades Scotland
properties.attribution: Nicolas Denizot, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

properties.title: Edward VI dies Greenwich England 1553AD
properties.content: in the year 1553AD after only 6 years of rule Edward VI dies
properties.attribution: Edward VI of England, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

properties.title: Jane Grey is briefly crowned queen London England 1553AD
properties.content: in the year 1553AD in desperate search for a protestant to inherit the crown lady Jane Grey is briefly crowned queen
properties.attribution: This work is in the public domain in its country of origin and other countries and areas where the copyright term is the author's life plus 100 years or fewer.

properties.title: Jane Grey relinquishes throne England 1553AD
properties.content: in the year 1553AD after just a few days being queen as a result of public outcryJane Grey Relinquishes the throne to "Bloody" Mary
properties.attribution: John Byam Liston Shaw, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

properties.title: Mary is crowned queen Westminster Abbey England 1553AD
properties.content: in the year 1553 "Bloody" Mary is crowned queen at Westminster Abbey
properties.attribution: Antonis Mor, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

properties.title: Jane Grey is beheaded Tower of London England 1554AD
properties.content: in the year 1554AD as a result of her attempt to take the throne "Bloody" Mary beheads Jane Grey at the Tower of London
properties.attribution: The author died in 1856, so this work is in the public domain in its country of origin and other countries and areas where the copyright term is the author's life plus 100 years or fewer.

properties.title: Thomas Wyatt rebels against Mary Kent England 1554AD
properties.content: in the year 1554AD in Kent England Thomas Wyatt launches a rebellion against Mary
properties.attribution: Nilfanion, CC BY-SA 3.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/3.0>, via Wikimedia Commons

properties.title: Thomas Wyatt is beheaded Tower Hill England 1554AD
properties.content: in the year 1554AD after his failed rebelion Thomas Wyatt is beheaded at the Tower of London
properties.attribution: Hans Holbein the Younger, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

properties.title: Elizabeth is imprisoned for her role in rebellion Tower of London England 1554AD
properties.content: in the year 1554AD Elizabeth is imprisoned in the tower by her half sister Mary for two years due to her alleged role in Wyatt's revolt
properties.attribution: Acabashi, CC BY-SA 4.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/4.0>, via Wikimedia Commons

properties.title: Mary marries Philip Winchester Cathedral England 1554AD
properties.content: in the year 1554AD "Bloody" Mary marries prince Philip of Spain at Winchester Cathedral
properties.attribution: Royal Museums Greenwich, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

properties.title: Mary carries out the Marian persecutions England 1554-1558AD
properties.content: between the years 1554AD-1558AD Mary carries out the "Marian Persecutions" which was the trial and beheading of hundreds of protestants throughout england; gave her the name "Bloody Mary"
properties.attribution: https://www.johnfoxe.org/woodcuts/f1805ins5.gif, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

properties.title: Mary loses Calais Calais France 1558AD
properties.content: in the year 1558AD Mary loses Calais a french territory which was under english rule to France
properties.attribution: This file is licensed under the Creative Commons Attribution-Share Alike 4.0 International license.

properties.title: Mary dies St James Palace England 1558AD
properties.content: in the year 1558AD Mary dies at St James Palace
properties.attribution: AFineClaret, CC BY-SA 4.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/4.0>, via Wikimedia Commons

properties.title: Elizabeth I is corinated Westminster Abbey England 1559AD
properties.content: in the year 1559 Elizabeth is corinated at Westminster Abbey
properties.attribution: After Levina Teerlinc, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

properties.title: Elizabeth passes Elizabethan Religous Settlement England 1559AD
properties.content: in the Year 1559AD Elizabeth passes two acts known together as the "Elizabethan Religous Settlement" Making her head of the church of england and mandating church attendance
properties.attribution: Sir Symonds D'Ewes, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

properties.title: Elizabeth catches small pox England 1564AD
properties.content: In the year 1564AD Elizabeth Catches Small pox and almost dies
properties.attribution: Paul Delaroche, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

properties.title: Earls attempt to depose Elizabeth England 1569AD
properties.content: in the year 1569AD numerous earls attempt to revolt against Elizabeth and fail
properties.attribution: Attributed to John de Critz, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

properties.title: Pope Pius V excomunicated Elizabeth Vatican City Italy 1570AD
properties.content: in the year 1570AD Pope Pius V Excommunicates Elizabeth from the Vatican
properties.attribution: El Greco, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

properties.title: Elizabeth executes Mary Queen of Scots Northamptonshire England 1587AD
properties.content: in the year 1587AD after a failed coup against the crown Mary Queen of Scots is tried and executed for treason in Northamptonshire England
properties.attribution: National Portrait Gallery, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

properties.title: Elizabeth fights off Spanish Armada English Channel England 1588AD
properties.content: In the year 1588AD Elizabeth defeats the Spanish Aramada in the English Channel with the help from Francis Drake and other generals
properties.attribution: Philip James de Loutherbourg, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

properties.title: Elizabeth dies Richmond Palace England 1603AD
properties.content: in the year 1603AD Elizabeth dies unwed and childless at Richmond palace
properties.attribution: Jacob Truedson Demitz for Ristesson History, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons
